info.description: All requests should have suitable headers. For example, if JSON is supplied as the request entity, the request should incorporate a `Content-type: application/json` header. Bearer tokens will be provided to consumers of each environment and is required to be passed along as an `Authorization` header of the format `Bearer $token`. A bearer token for dev can be found here. Note that dev and staging environments send email only to **@atlassian.com** and **@atlassian.com.test-google-a.com** emails.

201.description:
Account created successfully. The response body will contain the user data (without password) following the SCIM format.
Please do not rely on the auto-generated schema example values in the yellow boxes below. They will not necessarily be valid bodies. One notable difference is the "atlassian" object actually needs to be specified as "urn:scim:schemas:extension:atlassian:1.0". The placeholder request bodies are valid reference points.

post.description: Users can be searched for by username only using a subset of the SCIM filtering syntax. Currently the only supported filters are: * **username** with **ew** and **eq** as supported filters. Note that **ew** filter only accepts values starting with **@**. * **id** with **eq** as supported filter. You can search by multiple ids composing this filter with **or** Restricting the set of returned attributes and sorting are not supported. Pagination parameters "count" and "startIndex" are optional and will default to 50 and 1 respectively. For more details on the syntax of search requests, see [3.2.2 of the SCIM spec](http://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-scim-api-07#section-3.2.2).

200.description: Search query executed successfully. A search result resource, as per the SCIM spec, will be returned. If the user is present, a search result with one user resource will be returned; if the user does not exist, a search result with no user resources will be returned.

post.description:
Users can be searched for by username only using a subset of the SCIM filtering syntax.
Currently the only supported field for filtering is **username** with **ew** and **eq** as supported filters.
**ew** filters can be combined using **or** to reset passwords for multiple domains.
Note that **ew** filter only accepts values starting with **@**.
For more details on the syntax of search requests, see [§3.2.2 of the SCIM spec](http://tools.ietf.org/html/draft-ietf-scim-api-07#section-3.2.2)

post.summary: Invite a user to create an account

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
* invitee - The email address to which the invitation is sent.
* inviter - A text field (typically a name or an email address) appearing in the email that is sent out, stating who the invitation was sent by.
* application - A string key for the application performing the request. Valid values come from this properties file.
* displayName - An optional plain text field used as the default value for the display name of the invited user on the invitation acceptance page. If the invitee already exists, this value is ignored.
* applicationName - An optional text field that says what the user has been invited to use. If omitted, this value will be determined based on the provided application.
* text - Extra information which consuming services can include to give the user extra context, augmenting the default text. This is plain text. See also html. If text and html are both present, text is ignored.
* html - Extra information which consuming services can include to give the user extra context, augmenting the default text. This is HTML using a restricted set of tags (<b>, <br>, <em>, <h*>, <i>, <li>, <ol>, <p>, <strong>, <ul>). See also text. If text and html are both present, text is ignored. This is the only field permitting markup.
* continueUrl - The URL which the user will be redirected to after completing the invitation flow.
* daysValidFor - An optional number of days for the invitation link to be valid. Omitting it will use the default value (7). Value must be between 1-1095 (inclusive).
* sender - An optional name to use as the sender of the email.
* subject - The subject of the email.
* template - An optional string describing an alternate template to use for the email. Default: aid_account_invite_adg
* existingUserTemplate - The email template used for emailing an existing user. Default: aid_account_welcome_adg
* templateParameters - A map of arbitrary key/value pairs that can be used by the email sending system.
* simplified - Whether the simplified signup flow should be used or not.

x-examples.default:
{
  "displayName" : "Emma the Eager",
  "application" : "megamanzero",
  "existingUserTemplate" : "world",
  "daysValidFor" : 7,
  "subject" : "You are invited!",
  "templateParameters" : {
    "key" : "value",
    "key2" : "value2"
  },
  "inviter" : "alana@acme.com",
  "continueUrl" : "http://megamanzero:8082/login",
  "applicationName" : "My Company's Megaman Zero",
  "text" : "Hey, you are invited",
  "invitee" : "emma@acme.com",
  "simplified" : false,
  "template" : "hello",
  "sender" : "Alane Aware"
}

post.summary: Initiate reset password flow for the user

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
* **email** - The user's email to initiate reset password flow for.
* **application** - A string key for the application performing the request. Valid values come from this [properties file](https://stash.atlassian.com/projects/AID/repos/aid-core/browse/src/main/resources/applicationNames.properties).
* **tenant** - Optional string representing tenant name for Cloud instance
* **continueUrl** - The URL to which the user will be redirected to after changing password.
* **template** - An optional string describing an alternate template to use for the email. Default: [mac_forgot_password_adg](https://extranet.atlassian.com/display/EML/Atlassian+ID+-+Password+Reset).
* **templateParameters** - A map of arbitrary key/value pairs that can be used by the email sending system. Not that those parameters are not encoded by Atlassian account so they should be encoded on caller side

post.summary: Verify the email address of an account

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
* **application** - A string key for the application performing the request. Valid values come from this [properties file](https://stash.atlassian.com/projects/AID/repos/aid-core/browse/src/main/resources/applicationNames.properties).
* **username** - The username to request verification for. Note that currently there is a one-to-one correspondence between email addresses and usernames.
* **continueUrl** - The URL to which the user will be redirected to after verifying their email address.
* **text** - Extra information which consuming services can include to give the user extra context, augmenting the default text.
* **html** - Extra information which consuming services can include to give the user extra context, augmenting the default text. This is HTML using a restricted set of tags (`<b>`, `<br>`, `<em>`, `<h*>`, `<i>`, `<li>`, `<ol>`, `<p>`, `<strong>`, `<ul>`). This is the only field permitting markup.
* **sender** - An optional name to use as the sender of the email.
* **template** - An optional string describing an alternate template to use for the email. Default: [aid_account_verification_adg](https://extranet.atlassian.com/display/EML/Atlassian+ID+-+Email+Confirmation).
